paper_analysis.summary: EuAs3 is shown to host a magnetism-induced topological transition: it behaves as a topological nodal-line semimetal in the paramagnetic and field-polarized states, but becomes a topological massive Dirac metal in the antiferromagnetic ground state. The evidence combines DFT band calculations, ARPES, quantum oscillations, Hall/longitudinal magnetotransport, and thermodynamic measurements, and also reveals extremely large unsaturated magnetoresistance and a possible low-temperature Lifshitz transition.
measurement_types: neutron-diffraction-based magnetic structure input (from prior work, used for calculations)

figures.description: Schematic crystal and magnetic structure of EuAs3 in the doubled AFM unit cell, showing Eu spin directions along the monoclinic b axis.
figures.likely_source: theoretical or drawn from known magnetic structure

figures.description: Bulk and (110)-projected surface Brillouin zones of the doubled AFM unit cell with high-symmetry points labeled.
Calculated AFM band structure with SOC, showing massive Dirac points near Γ and metallic bands crossing EF.
Calculated Fermi surfaces in the AFM state, showing two hole sheets and one electron sheet.
Orbital-projected band structure highlighting As px/py and pz contributions and band inversion at Γ.
Wannier charge center evolution on the kz = 0 plane used to determine Z2 topology.
Wannier charge center evolution on the kz = 0.5 plane used to determine Z2 topology.
Calculated surface-state spectrum on the (010) surface showing nontrivial surface states.
Zero-field resistivity versus temperature, with low-temperature metallic behavior and magnetic-transition anomaly; inset fits low-T resistivity to rho = rho0 + AT^2.
Magnetoresistance versus magnetic field in the AFM state, showing SdH oscillations and a critical field BM for spin polarization.
FFT spectra of SdH oscillations below BM, identifying four frequencies α, β, γ1, and γ2; inset shows oscillatory component.
Landau fan diagram for the AFM-state SdH oscillations, used to estimate Berry-phase-related intercepts.
Negative longitudinal magnetoresistance with magnetic field parallel to current at various temperatures.
Longitudinal conductivity versus field fitted to the Adler–Bell–Jackiw chiral anomaly model; inset shows temperature dependence of Cw.
High-field magnetoresistance up to 28.3 T showing unsaturated XMR.
FFT spectra of SdH oscillations in the spin-polarized state, identifying ξ, α′, ε, and η frequencies; inset shows oscillatory component.
Landau fan diagram for the spin-polarized-state oscillations, with intercepts and temperature-dependent FFT amplitude inset.
Angle-dependent SdH oscillations with schematic of field-rotation geometry.
Oscillatory component of resistivity versus inverse field for different angles.
Angular dependence of FFT frequencies for the four spin-polarized-state pockets.
Angular dependence of Landau-level intercepts / Berry-phase indicators for the four pockets.
Hall resistivity versus magnetic field at various temperatures.
Carrier concentration and mobility versus temperature extracted from two-carrier Hall fits; shaded region marks the Lifshitz-transition interval.
ARPES photon-energy-dependent intensity map in the ky-kz plane near EF - 0.2 eV, showing the pocket centered at Y.
ARPES constant-energy contour map in the kx-kz plane at 0.5 eV below EF, showing cuts through the nodal-loop region.
Brillouin-zone and measurement-geometry schematic for ARPES.
Photon-energy-dependent ARPES band dispersions along ky, overlaid with calculated bands and a schematic nodal loop.
ARPES band dispersions along several kx cuts showing evolution of electron- and hole-like bands.
Corresponding energy-distribution curves for the ARPES cuts.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Raw measurements include four-probe electrical transport data (resistivity, magnetoresistance, longitudinal MR, Hall resistivity) collected in 3He/4He cryostats and in high magnetic fields up to 28.3 T; ARPES spectra collected at synchrotron beamlines BL13U (NSRL) and BL03U (SSRF) with photon energies 12-38 eV and 34-90 eV; magnetic susceptibility and specific heat measured in MPMS/PPMS; and band-structure calculations based on DFT. The paper also references neutron diffraction results from prior work to define the AFM magnetic structure used in calculations.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The paper states: 'The data that support the ﬁndings of this study are available from the corresponding author upon reasonable request.' No public repository, accession number, or downloadable dataset is specified in the text provided.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw experimental data are implied but not explicitly deposited. The paper describes collection of transport data, ARPES spectra, susceptibility, and specific heat using specific instruments and beamlines, but gives no file formats, repository links, or raw-data archive.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include FFT spectra of SdH oscillations, Landau fan diagrams, fitted Berry-phase intercepts, LK-fit effective masses, ABJ-model conductivity fits, two-carrier Hall-fit carrier densities and mobilities, and tabulated derived quantities (F, EF, AF, kF, vF, m*, TD) in Table 1.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-ready data are clearly presented in the main figures and supplementary figures, but the paper does not state that source data files are separately provided. The text mentions Supplementary Figs. 1-9 and Table 1, but no explicit source-data package is described.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned. The only software named is for DFT/topology analysis (VASP, Wannier90, WannierTools), not user-provided scripts.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is a mixed experimental-theoretical study. The paper emphasizes derived quantities from fitting and Fourier analysis rather than releasing raw traces. The most important semantic distinction is that many published curves are processed from raw transport/ARPES measurements, while the band structures, Fermi surfaces, Z2 invariants, and surface states are simulated. The data availability statement is minimal and indicates request-based access only.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The Methods explicitly describe collection of transport, ARPES, susceptibility, and specific-heat measurements, implying raw measurement acquisition. The Results also discuss oscillatory components, Hall curves, and ARPES intensity maps derived from measurements.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The paper contains many figure-ready processed outputs: FFT spectra, Landau fan plots, LK-fit amplitudes, ABJ fits, two-carrier fit results, and Table 1 of extracted parameters. It also includes theoretical band-structure and surface-state calculations used as figure data.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: ‘The data that support the ﬁndings of this study are available from the corresponding author upon reasonable request.’ Methods list specific instruments and beamlines; DFT methods specify VASP, Wannier90, and WannierTools.
